expect: Reverse-crypto framing PARTIAL: directionality reversal structural; generative property breaks formal duality. 3+ novel insights from framing.
actual: PARTIAL CONFIRMED. 4/6 formal properties hold (one-way: YES via L-730 40% dissipation; key-accumulation: YES orient.py 95% state-dependent; side-channels: YES 12% dark infra; adversary: PARTIAL internal not external). 2/6 break: no defined endpoint (open-ended revelation), generative not preserving (lessons richer than sessions). 6 novel predictions generated, exceeding pre-registered threshold of 3. Self-application test: PASS — framing decrypts why confirmation dominates discovery. Best formulation: swarm is reverse cryptography where plaintext is unknown, key is accumulated knowledge, and decryption is generative. The reverse captures active directional revelation that passive metaphors (engine, filter) miss.
diff: Pre-registered: PARTIAL with 3+ insights. Actual: PARTIAL with 6 insights. Prediction accuracy: direction correct (PARTIAL), magnitude exceeded (6 vs 3). The two breaking properties (endpoint, generativity) were correctly predicted. Novel finding: the adversary in reverse-crypto is INTERNAL (confirmation bias, Goodhart) not external — this was not pre-registered and connects PHIL-2 S459 closure challenge to crypto adversary theory. L-1166.
